Type BFManual() to open the manual.

library(BayesianId)
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), scale))
levels(test_data$island)
[1] "Biscoe"    "Dream"     "Torgersen"
nc = length(levels(test_data$island)) - 1
contrasts(test_data$island) = contr_banova(nc + 1)
contrasts(test_data$island)
                [,1]       [,2]
Biscoe     0.8164966  0.0000000
Dream     -0.4082483  0.7071068
Torgersen -0.4082483 -0.7071068
X = model.matrix(~ flipper_len + island, data = test_data)
head(X)
  (Intercept) flipper_len    island1    island2
1           1  -1.4246077 -0.4082483 -0.7071068
2           1  -1.0678666 -0.4082483 -0.7071068
3           1  -0.4257325 -0.4082483 -0.7071068
5           1  -0.5684290 -0.4082483 -0.7071068
6           1  -0.7824736 -0.4082483 -0.7071068
7           1  -1.4246077 -0.4082483 -0.7071068
colMeans(X)
 (Intercept)  flipper_len      island1      island2 
1.000000e+00 2.808064e-16 1.912515e-01 1.613817e-01 
prep = data.frame(y = c(-2, 9, 1, 8, -3, 1, 7),
                       x1 = factor(c("a", "b", "b", "a", "c", "a", "c")),
                       x2 = c(1, 2, 3, 1, 1, 0, 2),
                       x = c(-1.4, 0.1, 0.8, -0.9, 0.5, -2.4, 12.0)) %>%
  prepare_data(formula = y ~ x1 + x2 + x)
  #prepare_data(formula = y ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + x1:x2 + x2:x3)

X = model.matrix(y ~ x1 + x2 + x,
                 data = prep$data)
head(X)
  (Intercept)        x11        x12         x2          x
1           1  0.8164966  0.0000000 -0.4285714 -2.6428571
2           1 -0.4082483  0.7071068  0.5714286 -1.1428571
3           1 -0.4082483  0.7071068  1.5714286 -0.4428571
4           1  0.8164966  0.0000000 -0.4285714 -2.1428571
5           1 -0.4082483 -0.7071068 -0.4285714 -0.7428571
6           1  0.8164966  0.0000000 -1.4285714 -3.6428571
head(penguins)
# initialize prior adjustment vector
prior_adjust = rep(1.0, times = ncol(X))
names(prior_adjust) = colnames(X)

# loop through numeric predictor variables and adjust the prior by their SDs
# this is equivalent to z-scoring the numeric predictors
for(i in 1:length(prep$x_numeric_names)){ 
  x_name = prep$x_numeric_names[i] # current numeric predictor variables
  has_x = grepl(x_name, colnames(X)) # Is the predictor part of each column in the design matrix?
  print(x_name)
  names(has_x) = colnames(X)
  print(has_x)
  print("")
  prior_adjust[has_x] = prior_adjust[has_x]/sd(X[,x_name]) # if so, adjust for that predictor's standard deviation
}
[1] "x2"
(Intercept)         x11         x12          x2           x 
      FALSE       FALSE       FALSE        TRUE       FALSE 
[1] ""
[1] "x"
(Intercept)         x11         x12          x2           x

[1] ""

Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).

library(lme4)
fit_lmer = lmer(body_mass ~ flipper_len*bill_len + (1 | island), 
                data = test_data,
                REML = FALSE)
results = inc_tests(fit_lmer)
results$inc_tabl
vcov(results$fit_list[[1]])

print(fit_blmer)
test_marginality = function(terms){
  is_interaction = grepl("\\:", terms)
  all_okay = terms[is_interaction] |> sapply(function(intr){has_needed(terms, intr)}) |> all()
  return(all_okay)
}
is_interaction = grepl("\\:", terms)
terms[is_interaction] |> sapply(function(intr){has_needed(terms, intr)})
  a:b   b:c a:b:c 
 TRUE  TRUE FALSE 
test_marginality(c("a", "b", "c", "a:b", "b:c", "a:c", "a:b:c")) |> print()
[1] TRUE
test_marginality(c("a", "b", "c", "a:b", "b:c", "a:b:c")) |> print()
[1] FALSE
has_needed = function(terms, intr){
  parts = strsplit(intr, "\\:") |> unlist()
  ord = length(parts)
  what_needed = combn(parts, ord - 1, simplify = FALSE) |> lapply(function(x){paste(x, collapse = ":")}) |> unlist()
  return(all(what_needed %in% terms))
}
has_needed(terms, "a:b")
